exemptions.reason: Generic context-menu event transport; concrete Latte-owned actions are inventoried at their QAction declarations.
Invisible tooltip host with no settings handler; the underlying wheel MouseArea owns the affordance.
Hover-only alternate value display; accepts no button and performs no write or action.
Hover-only pixel conversion display; accepts no button and performs no write or action.
Hover-only pixel conversion display; accepts no button and performs no write or action.
Viewport navigation supplied by Qt Controls, not a setting or Latte action.
Nonselectable visual separator in the Add/Duplicate popup.
Shared popup-row implementation; every semantic call-site row is inventoried separately.
Two equivalent hit hosts implement one semantic HeaderSwitch; call-site records require both label and switch input paths.
Hover and popup-lifecycle wrapper; the four child action buttons are separate affordances.
Visual drag handle has no input handler; the outer configurationArea owns reorder input.
Loads a plugin-declared page but is not an input control. Bundled Latte pages are inventoried; third-party page internals remain plugin-owned and require only the host contract.
Bundled indicator has no X-Latte-ConfigUi and therefore exposes no per-view configuration controls.
Commented-out QML is not instantiated or reachable.
Task/launcher drag-and-drop behavior is not selected by a per-view setting; configured click and wheel paths remain inventoried.
MPRIS playback controls operate application media and are independent of Latte per-view settings.
Launches the global Preferences surface, assigned to the later global-settings inventory.
Global layout switching and layout editing belong to the later global-settings phase; moving the current view between layouts is inventoried separately.
The Export Template dialog belongs to the later global-settings phase.
Application shutdown is not a settings affordance.
Debug-only message action is not a setting and is hidden by the context-menu policy.
Fixed applet activation, shortcut, geometry, and parabolic handlers are not selected by a per-view setting; the configured popup-wheel path remains inventoried separately.
Fixed task context actions for launchers, desktops, activities, applet editing, and window close are independent of the per-view showWindowActions-controlled More Actions submenu.
Nonselectable context-menu section headers and separators provide menu structure but execute no action.

The public shared SpinBox is shipped but has no live per-view settings instantiation. Its declaration is the 25th explicit exemption; any future use in a scanned settings surface creates its own unmapped candidate.
